title: paspalum aberrans
intro: Paspalum aberrans, also known as Paspalidium geminatum or water couch, is a type of perennial grass native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as skin infections and digestive problems. The health benefits of Paspalum aberrans include its ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use, and it can also be applied topically as a poultice or cream for external use.
